If you look at the way comedians create their great content, a lot of times these comedians that we see, the stand-up comedians, the way they create their content is it looks like they stand up and they're just the funny man. Right? Oh man, everything they say is amazing. Everything they say is fantastic. Look how funny they are. Okay, but the reality is that's not the way it is. And they do their skits. They do the stage. And can everybody hear me okay, first of all? Can you go and drop on in? You guys can hear me okay? Okay, sweet, appreciate it. Okay, but these comedians go out and they stay there a bit and two of the 10 jokes will be awesome. Okay, the other jokes will suck. Okay, and they don't do them again. So now they got two good jokes. They'll go out and they'll rewrite eight. They'll go to the next bar in the next place. Do the original two plus eight new ones, maybe a new one sticks. Okay, and they do that process until they go in, until they have this amazing thing. And they're using the market to give them feedback on a good stage show. Okay, and that's how they do it. Very much the same way for the way that we create the things that we do. Okay, we go out and we create funnel after funnel after funnel after funnel. We're doing them quickly. We're not trying to get lost in the details. We're launching them quickly and tossing them out there to see what stays on the wall and what drops down. Okay, the biggest reason though that someone doesn't go ahead and be successful with this usually is because they just quit too early and they stop too fast. And gain the confidence to do that in your life. And that's what makes it happen. Okay. That's what makes it move forward. Now I understand why they call Steven the drill sergeant. Do it, do it Lars. Yeah. That's right. Hey guys, but that's the whole point of it. That's the point of today's lesson. So go ahead and watch Russell's thing. And then if you, one last little lesson here. If you watch the way I launched that first infill product of mine and why it failed so bad, it's because what I did is I first built a funnel and then I was like, oh man, check, check this funnel out. This funnel is so cool. What should I sell? And then I came up with my offer and then I was like, how should I sell it? How should I sell that offer? And then I came up with the sales message. Okay. That is exactly the wrong order to build in, but it is what 99% of people do when they first get click, click, click funnels, they go build a funnel. Then they're like, what should I build or what should I sell? Right. And then they say, how should I sell it? 100% success comes from doing the exact 100% offset to that. Okay. The way that this challenge will lace forward as we move forward into week one. First, what we're going to do is we're going to teach you how to sell. Okay. And, and, and how to actually create value. Then we're going to go and we're going to teach you how to create an offer. Then we're going to teach you how to create the funnel. The funnel is the easiest part. Click funnels. It makes it so easy. It's sexy. So everyone just skips to it. It's the exact opposite way. Okay. Don't just jump in and start building the funnel. Okay. First, we're going to craft a message that causes value and people who want to give you money. Then the offer fulfills on the promise your sales message made. The funnel is just how it's delivered. That's it. Okay. It really, the better you are at sales message and offer creation, the better it is that you're going to be at this game. That's why we don't just jump straight into click funnels. Okay. You need to understand those pieces in that order. And when you do it that way, really, really, really easy to make cash, really easy to make cash. I spent the first several years though doing it the opposite way. I was like, why isn't it working? Why isn't it working? Why isn't it working? When I did the exact opposite though, it was insanely amazing how fat the wallet got. Okay. And because if I make a funnel, I make an offer based on the funnel I built. That's scary. If I then make my offer, I make a sales message based on the offer I created. That's the wrong order. Does that make sense? You end up making it with constraints based on what you built. If I do the other way though, and this is what we're going to teach you guys. Okay. I'm going to build a sales message that gets people off their butts because people are naturally lazy and that's okay. But I'm going to go and I'm going to create a sales message that causes value. And they're like, crap, this is awesome. Right. I need to buy something from you know, I have those urges, right? We're going to teach you how to create that. Then I create an offer that fulfills on the message. Okay. Because you can try a lot of sales messages and they're not going to work, not going to work, not going to work, not going to work. And then suddenly one hits. What did I promise on that one? Do they really like that the market is saying they're willing to pay for? Sweet. Let me go make that. Okay. Then let me create the funnel that delivers all of it. Okay. Way easier. So that's what you guys are going to see as we move forward inside this challenge. That's why we did it the way it is and that's why it's in the order that it's in. There was a panel of millionaires. Okay. There was a panel of millionaires and they were all on on on stage in front of this big audience. Okay. And what was cool was they started asking every one of the millionaires how many times they launched a business before they made a lot of money and they went boom, boom, through the next one, through the next one, through the next one, through the next one. Right. And they all said anywhere, I think if the average was 11 to like 14 business tries before one of them actually made cash. Okay. I went through 12 before I made money, before I understood what funnels were and then it was only like two or three tries and it was more money than I was currently making at my job.
